original_description: Audit and gate the skill-tree against three principles: holistic integration, closing the loop, and baseline-enrichment. Two modes: audit (full tree scan) and gate (post-skill-creator validation). Produces infrastructure-mapped recommendations gated by simplify + eval-protocol, with automatic seeds issue creation. Triggers: "audit skill tree", "check principles", "skill tree health", "autoresearch audit", "/autoresearch", or after skill-creator writes a SKILL.md (via PostToolUse hook). Do NOT trigger for: running A/B tests directly (run_ab.py, grade.py), debugging, code review, or general codebase exploration.
best_description: Audit and gate the skill-tree against three principles: holistic integration, closing the loop, and baseline-enrichment. Two modes: audit (full tree scan) and gate (post-skill-creator validation). Produces infrastructure-mapped recommendations gated by simplify + eval-protocol, with automatic seeds issue creation. Triggers: "audit skill tree", "check principles", "skill tree health", "autoresearch audit", "/autoresearch", or after skill-creator writes a SKILL.md (via PostToolUse hook). Do NOT trigger for: running A/B tests directly (run_ab.py, grade.py), debugging, code review, or general codebase exploration.
final_description: Use for skill-tree quality enforcement: auditing all skills against three principles (holistic integration, closing the loop, baseline-enrichment), or validating a newly created skill against those same principles. Two entry points: user requests a skill-tree audit/health check, OR a skill was just created/modified with skill-creator and needs principle validation before it's considered done. Produces concrete infrastructure recommendations (cross-references, eval checkpoints, diffusion targets) filtered through a quality gate, with seeds issues for gaps found. Do NOT use for: running A/B experiments directly, debugging hooks, code review, or general codebase exploration.

history.description: Audit and gate the skill-tree against three principles: holistic integration, closing the loop, and baseline-enrichment. Two modes: audit (full tree scan) and gate (post-skill-creator validation). Produces infrastructure-mapped recommendations gated by simplify + eval-protocol, with automatic seeds issue creation. Triggers: "audit skill tree", "check principles", "skill tree health", "autoresearch audit", "/autoresearch", or after skill-creator writes a SKILL.md (via PostToolUse hook). Do NOT trigger for: running A/B tests directly (run_ab.py, grade.py), debugging, code review, or general codebase exploration.

train_results.query: audit the skill tree
I just added a new skill with skill-creator, can you validate it against the three principles?
skill tree health check
check principles compliance across all skills, I think some are missing baseline enrichment hooks
do a full audit of ~/.claude/skills to see what's missing or out of alignment
run autoresearch on the skill tree
run grade.py on the last eval round and show me the scores
my eval-protocol gates aren't firing correctly after a skill runs — debug the PostToolUse hook
generate a changelog from git history and check which features are missing documentation
I want to stress test the new /payments API endpoint I just built, try to find vulnerabilities
audit the codebase architecture and map the subsystems for the new payment service
check the SKILL.md I just wrote for skill-creator to see if the trigger description is clear enough

test_results.query: check if my skills follow the holistic integration principle
my skill tree feels fragmented, can you check whether the skills close the loop on each other
autoresearch gate — just wrote a SKILL.md for the new deploy skill, make sure it's up to standard
I just wrote a new skill file, review the code quality and simplify it
run run_ab.py with the new prompt variant and compare against baseline
check the skill I wrote for the brainstorming workflow — does the description make sense?
audit my approved bash commands in settings.json for anything dangerous
